description:
Goal
Make compiler2 native lowering and codegen consume the settled shared recursive runtime transport model so direct-only callables stay direct across input, call, return, and resume boundaries without allocating or publishing first-class closures.

Problem being fixed
This ticket does not own the recursive direct-callable capture model itself. The root bug is tracked in fz-hwn.10. Native was the first strict consumer to expose it, but the first wrong representation appears earlier in artifact transport derivation. Once fz-hwn.10 lands, native must stop rebuilding callable transport from flat capture lanes or value types and instead consume the recursive transport facts end to end.

Scope after fz-hwn.10
- Consume recursive runtime input/return/resume layouts in src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs without re-deriving callable capture shape.
- Keep native local values structurally aligned with the recursive transport model instead of storing direct callables as only function id plus flat lane vars.
- Keep direct-only callable lowering in direct-call form wherever the settled transport layout says the value remains internal and non-first-class.
- Materialize/publish first-class callable wrappers only where runtime demand explicitly requires them.
- Keep callable-entry ABI separate from executable-internal transport ABI.

Code seams
- src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs
- src/compiler2/native_codegen/*
- any remaining native/backend cleanup needed after flat capture assumptions are removed

Removal target
- Delete any remaining native helper that interprets direct-callable values primarily as flat capture-lane vectors.
- Delete any native fallback that recovers callable obligations from lambda/function-ref syntax or callable-looking types when recursive transport facts already exist.
- Delete temporary native-only internal layout shims once recursive transport is authoritative.

Checks
- Native JIT/AOT tests pass for direct lambda call, escaped lambda, opaque callable, enum reducer cases, quicksort, and direct closure-target fixtures.
- No native path emits MakeClosure/MakeFnRef for direct-only demand.
- No native path publishes callable boundaries from undemanded lambda/function-ref steps.
- Native lowering consumes the recursive transport model from fz-hwn.10 without reintroducing flat-capture reconstruction.

comments.text: Current seam after mirroring demand-shaped local values into native executable inputs and entry captures: native direct-only callable transport still collapses at DeliveredResume because ReturnAbi can only express Value or TupleFields. Repro: compiler2_native_program_jit_runs_enum_reduce_through_compiler2_codegen fails in LowerNativeProgram with 'native call arg 7 for callee 66 expected direct-callable 65 with 0 capture(s), observed Some(Runtime(Var(7)))'. This indicates a direct-only callable returning through a continuation resume boundary and being rebound as a runtime value. Next correct step is to generalize internal return/resume transport from ReturnAbi::{Value,TupleFields} to the same value-layout model used for runtime params/captures, then thread that through artifact planning, backend interpreter DeliveredResume binding, native entry signatures, delivered_args_for_abi, and NativeDemandAbi/codegen delivered-shape handling. Do not restore old closure materialization to satisfy the test.

title: Compiler2 native: lower direct callable demand without first-class closure materialization
